results.seo_title: Implementing SHAP Explainability Workflows for Machine Learning…
results.meta_description: Learn how to implement SHAP explainability workflows with Explainer Comparisons, Maskers, Interactions, Drift, and Black-Box Models for machine learning model…

source.title: A Coding Guide Implementing SHAP Explainability Workflows with Explainer Comparisons, Maskers, Interactions, Drift, and Black-Box Models
source.summary:
In this tutorial, we implement SHAP workflows as a practical framework for interpreting machine learning models beyond basic feature-importance plots. We start by training tree-based models and then compare different SHAP explainers, including Tree, Exact, Permutation, and Kernel methods, to understand how accuracy and runtime change across model-aware and model-agnostic approaches. We also examine how […]

The post A Coding Guide Implementing SHAP Explainability Workflows with Explainer Comparisons, Maskers, Interactions, Drift, and Black-Box Models appeared first on MarkTechPost.

results.seo_title: DynFrame: Adaptive Reasoning-Driven Multimodal Framework for Complex…
results.meta_description: DynFrame is a framework that addresses structural gaps in existing thinking-with-video systems by emitting temporal window and sampling density as native…
results.body_html:
DynFrame is a framework that addresses structural gaps in existing thinking-with-video systems by emitting temporal window and sampling density as native tokens within a single autoregressive pass.

Emits temporal window and sampling density as native tokens within a single autoregressive pass Enables acquiring multi-granularity evidence with a single retrieval step Splits each rollout at the retrieval boundary and assigns role-specific token-level advantages Trained on curated DM-CoT-74k and DM-RL-45k datasets, achieving competitive performance with strong baselines

Advantages of DynFrame over Existing Methods

Sampling Density DynFrame's learnable span-density retrieval enables acquiring multi-granularity evidence with a single retrieval step

Temporal Window DynFrame emits temporal window as native tokens, allowing for more efficient and effective video understanding

Benchmarks and Results Trained on the curated DM-CoT-74k and DM-RL-45k datasets, DynFrame-4B is competitive with strong 7B-8B baselines across six benchmarks (NExT-GQA, Charades-STA, ActivityNet-MR, Video-MME, MLVU, LVBench), and DynFrame-8B sets new state-of-the-art on most metrics.

source.title: DynFrame: Adaptive Reasoning-Driven Multimodal Framework with Dynamic Frame Augmentation for Complex Video Understanding
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new framework called DynFrame, which aims to improve the performance of video understanding models by addressing two structural gaps in existing thinking-with-video systems. The framework uses a learnable span-density retrieval mechanism that enables acquiring multi-granularity evidence with a single retrieval step. DynFrame has been trained on large datasets and achieved competitive results with strong baselines across six benchmarks, setting a new state-of-the-art on most metrics.

results.seo_title: Compress and Benchmark LLMs with FP8, GPTQ, and SmoothQuant…
results.meta_description: Learn how to compress and benchmark instruction-tuned LLMs with FP8, GPTQ, and SmoothQuant quantization using llmcompressor.
results.body_html:
Can you compress and benchmark LLMs with FP8, GPTQ, and SmoothQuant quantization using llmcompressor? Yes, with this tutorial.

Step 1: Install llmcompressor

  • Download the llmcompressor repository from GitHub.
  • Clone the repository using git clone https://github.com/MarkTechPost/llmcompressor.git.
  • Change into the cloned directory and run pip install -r requirements.txt.

Step 2: Prepare your LLM model

Prepare your instruction-tuned language model by downloading the pre-trained weights.

Model NamePre-trained Weights
FP16 BaselineYour FP16 baseline model
GPTQ W4A16Your GPTQ W4A16 model
SmoothQuant with GPTQ W8A8Your SmoothQuant with GPTQ W8A8 model

Step 3: Compress and benchmark your LLM model

Use llmcompressor compress --model your-llm-model to compress your LLM model.

source.title: A Coding Implementation to Compress and Benchmark Instruction-Tuned LLMs with FP8, GPTQ, and SmoothQuant Quantization using llmcompressor
source.summary:
In this tutorial, we explore how to apply post-training quantization to an instruction-tuned language model using llmcompressor. We start with an FP16 baseline and then compare multiple compression strategies, including FP8 dynamic quantization, GPTQ W4A16, and SmoothQuant with GPTQ W8A8. Along the way, we benchmark each model variant for disk size, generation latency, throughput, perplexity, […]

The post A Coding Implementation to Compress and Benchmark Instruction-Tuned LLMs with FP8, GPTQ, and SmoothQuant Quantization using llmcompressor appeared first on MarkTechPost.

results.seo_title: The AI Fight Brewing Inside The New York Times
results.meta_description: The New York Times employees are gearing up for a fight over the use of AI in the newsroom, with concerns about job security and performance metrics.
results.body_html:
The New York Times employees are gearing up for a fight over the use of AI in the newsroom, with concerns about job security and performance metrics.

How AI is being used at The New York Times

  • DX, an internal AI tool, tracks employee output, generative AI use, and efficiency, but its personalized benchmarks have raised concerns about job security.
  • The union has filed grievances saying the company violated their collective bargaining agreement when it started using DX.

What are the implications of AI on journalism?

ImplicationsDescription
Job securityThe use of AI in the newsroom has raised concerns about job security, particularly for those working in data analysis and other roles.
Benchmarks and metricsThe personalized benchmarks from DX have been criticized for flattening all work into an opaque set of metrics that can be used against staff in disciplinary or performance review settings.

What is the union's stance on AI use?

“Now people in disciplinary situations are suddenly having read back to them, ‘You only did one [pull request] per week, per whatever, and that’s 25 percent below industry standard,’” says Ben Harnett, a software engineer at The New York Times.

The union has filed an unfair labor practice charge earlier this month, highlighting the need for greater transparency around AI use in the newsroom.

source.title: The AI fight brewing inside The New York Times
source.summary: The New York Times is facing a labor dispute with its employees over the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in newsrooms. The debate on how to integrate AI into journalism has been ongoing within the industry, and now union representatives are negotiating with publishers to establish guidelines for its use. The outcome of these negotiations will determine how AI is used in the newspaper's operations and what impact it may have on jobs and editorial standards.

results.seo_title: SpaceX's Starlink Wins American Airlines Contract
results.meta_description: American Airlines plans to install Starlink on over 500 Airbus aircraft, a win for SpaceX's IPO.
results.body_html:
SpaceX's Starlink has won another major contract, this time with American Airlines. The airline plans to install Starlink on over 500 Airbus aircraft, a significant win for SpaceX ahead of its IPO.

Key Details

  • Number of aircraft: Over 500 Airbus planes
  • Carrier win: Latest contract for SpaceX's IPO-bound company
  • Installation plan: To be completed in phases

Impact on Industry

The installation of Starlink on American Airlines' fleet is expected to have a significant impact on the airline's operations and customer experience.

BenefitDescription
Improved in-flight connectivityStarlink will provide faster and more reliable internet access for passengers
Enhanced safety featuresThe system will enable pilots to stay connected with air traffic control during flights

Technical Specifications

Starlink will be installed on Airbus A320 and A321 aircraft, with the installation process expected to take several months.

source.title: SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
source.summary: SpaceX's Starlink satellite internet service has secured a contract with American Airlines to be installed on over 500 of its Airbus aircraft. This is another significant win for SpaceX ahead of its upcoming initial public offering (IPO). The deal marks an expansion of Starlink's reach into the aviation industry.

results.seo_title: BoxLitE: A Faithful Knowledge Base Embedding Based on Convex…
results.meta_description: A new KB embedding model for DL-Lite that leverages convex optimization to achieve faithful models.
results.body_html:
What is BoxLitE? BoxLitE is a new KB embedding model for DL-Lite that leverages convex optimization to achieve faithful models.

Key Features of BoxLitEAllows for convex optimizationGeneralizes information present in facts and conceptual knowledgeRepresent hierarchies using convex regionsAdvantages of BoxLitEAdvantageDescriptionWeakly faithful modelBoxLitE achieves weakly faithful models for any satisfiable DL-Lite KBImplementation and Future WorkFormulate the KB embedding task as a convex optimization problem and obtain embeddings with desirable faithfulness properties.

source.title: BoxLitE: A Faithful Knowledge Base Embedding Based on Convex Optimization
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have introduced BoxLitE, a new knowledge base embedding model that uses convex optimization to represent hierarchies in ontologies. The model aims to combine the capabilities of classical knowledge graph embeddings with conceptual knowledge from TBoxes, allowing for more general concepts to be mapped to larger regions. This approach enables the representation of complex relationships between concepts and can lead to more faithful models. BoxLitE has been shown to produce weakly faithful embeddings for satisfiable DL-Lite KBs.

results.seo_title: Always Learning, Always Mixing: Efficient and Simple Data Mixing All…
results.meta_description: Discover OP-Mix, an efficient data mixing algorithm that improves language model training quality and reduces compute requirements.
results.body_html:
What is data mixing in language model training? Data mixing decides how to combine different sources or types of data and is a consequential problem throughout language model training.

Key Insight: Simulating Candidate Data Mixtures Our main insight is that candidate data mixtures can be cheaply simulated by interpolating between low-rank adapters trained directly on the current model, eliminating separate proxy models and ensuring the search is always grounded in the model's actual learning dynamics.

Eliminates need for separate proxy models Simulates candidate data mixtures using low-rank adapters Ensures search is grounded in model's actual learning dynamics

Advantages of OP-Mix OP-Mix consistently finds near-optimal mixtures while using a fraction of the compute of the baselines.

Pretraining performance improvement 6.3% average perplexity gain N/A

Compute requirements for continual learning 66% less overall compute 95% more overall compute

Impact on Language Model Training OP-Mix suggests a different view of language model training: not a sequence of distinct phases, but a single continuous process of learning from data.

OP-Mix improves upon training without mixing by 6.3% in average perplexity, and matches the performance of both retraining and on-policy distillation while using 66% and 95% less overall compute, respectively.

source.title: Always Learning, Always Mixing: Efficient and Simple Data Mixing All The Time
source.summary: Data mixing decides how to combine different sources or types of data and is a consequential problem throughout language model training. In pretraining, data composition is a key determinant of model quality; in continual learning and adaptation, it governs what is retained and acquired. Yet existing data mixing methods address only one phase of this lifecycle at a time: some require smaller proxy models tied to a single training phase, others assume a fixed domain set, and continual learning lacks principled guidance altogether. We argue that data mixing is fundamentally an online decision making problem -- one that recurs throughout training and demands a single, unified solution. We introduce OP-Mix (On-Policy Mix), a data mixing algorithm that operates across the entire language model training lifecycle. Our main insight is that candidate data mixtures can be cheaply simulated by interpolating between low-rank adapters trained directly on the current model, eliminating separate proxy models and ensuring the search is always grounded in the model's actual learning dynamics. Across pretraining, continual midtraining, and continual instruction tuning, OP-Mix consistently finds near-optimal mixtures while using a fraction of the compute of the baselines. In pretraining, OP-Mix improves upon training without mixing by 6.3% in average perplexity. For continual learning, OP-Mix matches the performance of both retraining and on-policy distillation while using 66% and 95% less overall compute, respectively. OP-Mix suggests a different view of language model training: not a sequence of distinct phases, but a single continuous process of learning from data.

Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A tutorial has been published that guides users to build and compare FedAvg and FedProx federated learning models using NVIDIA FLARE on a non-IID CIFAR-10 dataset. The tutorial simulates realistic label imbalance across federated sites by splitting client data using a Dirichlet distribution. results.seo_title: 6G Communication Networks Enabling Embodied Agents: Architecture and…
results.meta_description: This article investigates 6G-enabled communication systems for embodied agents from both conceptual and engineering perspectives.
results.body_html:
6G promises sub-millisecond latency, ultra-high reliability, native intelligence, and integrated sensing for embodied agent communication.

Key Enablers of Human-Robot Interaction

Sub-millisecond latency Ultra-high reliability Native intelligence Integrated sensing

Symbiotic Relationship between Embodied Agents and 6G Networks

Coverage Extension Embodied agents can extend coverage through physical world understanding.

Environmental Sensing Embodied agents can sense their environment and provide feedback to humans.

Physical World Understanding Embodied agents can understand the physical world and interact with it.

Hierarchical Communication Architecture for Human-Robot Remote Interaction Human-intent perception layer, open radio access network (O-RAN)-based transport layer, intelligent intermediary layer, and embodiment layer.

Experimental Results and Validation

Millisecond-level latency Confirmed through experimental results.

Stable closed-loop operation Confirmed through experimental results.
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: 6G Communication Networks Enabling Embodied Agents: Architecture and Prototype
source.summary: Researchers have developed a hierarchical communication architecture for human-robot remote interaction using 6G-enabled networks. The proposed system, which includes a human-intent perception layer, an open radio access network-based transport layer, an intelligent intermediary layer, and an embodiment layer, aims to support the stringent requirements of human-robot interaction with sub-millisecond latency and ultra-high reliability. An end-to-end prototype was implemented using a haptic device, industrial robotic arm, intermediary platform, and 5G O-RAN testbed, demonstrating millisecond-level latency and stable closed-loop operation.

Advanced AI systems are vulnerable to cybersecurity threats, and experts must identify necessary defenses to prioritize them.

Our framework enables cybersecurity experts to identify which defenses are necessary—and how to prioritize them By analyzing potential vulnerabilities, experts can develop targeted mitigation strategies The framework provides a comprehensive understanding of the threat landscape, enabling informed decision-making

Key Considerations for Defense Prioritization

Defense Category Prioritization Level

Best Practices for Mitigation Strategies

Regularly update and patch AI systems to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities Implement robust access controls, including multi-factor authentication Conduct regular security audits to identify potential weaknesses

Conclusion The evaluation of potential cybersecurity threats for advanced AI systems is a critical task that requires careful consideration of defense prioritization and mitigation strategies.

source.title: Evaluating potential cybersecurity threats of advanced AI
source.summary: Our framework enables cybersecurity experts to identify which defenses are necessary—and how to prioritize them

results.seo_title: MCP for Research: How to Connect AI to Research Tools
results.meta_description: Learn how to connect AI to research tools using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and automate research discovery.
results.body_html:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that allows agentic models to communicate with external tools and data sources. For research discovery, this means AI can use research tools through natural language requests, automating platform switching and cross-referencing.

Research Discovery: Three Layers of Abstraction

Much like software development, research discovery can be framed in terms of layers of abstraction.

  • Manual Research: At the lowest level of abstraction, researchers search manually and cross-reference by hand.
  • Scripted Tools: Python scripts automate research discovery by handling web requests, parsing responses, and organizing results.
  • MCP Integration: MCP makes these same Python tools accessible to AI systems through natural language.

Setup and Usage

The easiest way to add the Research Tracker MCP is through Hugging Face MCP Settings:

  • Search for "research-tracker-mcp" in the available tools
  • Follow the provided setup instructions for your specific client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, VS Code, etc.)

Benefits of MCP Integration

The AI orchestrates multiple tools, fills information gaps, and reasons about results:

Example research directive: Find recent transformer architecture papers published in the last 6 months: - Must have available implementation code - Focus on papers with pretrained models - Include performance benchmarks when available

This can be viewed as an additional layer of abstraction above scripting, where the "programming language" is natural language.

Understanding the lower layers (both manual and scripted) leads to better implementations.

source.title: MCP for Research: How to Connect AI to Research Tools
source.summary: MCP for Research: How to Connect AI to Research Tools

results.seo_title: Learning to Learn from Multimodal Experience: A New Paradigm for…
results.meta_description: Discover how adaptive memory design enhances agent performance and generalization in multimodal tasks, with a new paradigm for learning from multimodal…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The proposed paradigm for learning to learn from multimodal experience enables agents to dynamically construct, organize, and utilize memory based on task requirements and interaction history.

Task-dependent memory design Dynamically constructed memory Adaptive utilization of experience

Conclusion and Future Work Adaptive memory design substantially enhances agent performance and generalization across multimodal tasks, highlighting the critical role of learning memory mechanisms in experience-driven learning.

source.title: Learning to Learn from Multimodal Experience
source.summary: Experience-driven learning has emerged as a promising paradigm for enabling agents to improve from interaction trajectories by accumulating and reusing past experience. However, existing approaches are predominantly developed in textual settings and rely on manually designed memory schemas, limiting their applicability to multimodal environments. In real-world scenarios, experience is inherently multimodal, involving heterogeneous signals across perception, reasoning, and action, which makes effective memory design significantly more challenging. In particular, the optimal way to structure and utilize multimodal experience is highly task-dependent and evolves over time, rendering fixed memory designs insufficient. In this work, we propose a new paradigm, learning to learn from multimodal experience, which shifts memory design from a predefined component to an adaptive and learnable process. Our framework enables agents to dynamically construct, organize, and utilize memory based on task requirements and interaction history, effectively learning how to structure experience for improved performance. Experiments demonstrate that adaptive memory design substantially enhances agent performance and generalization across multimodal tasks, highlighting the critical role of learning memory mechanisms in experience-driven learning.

results.seo_title: Federated Nested Learning: Collaborative Training of…
results.meta_description: Learn how Federated Nested Learning (FedNL) tackles Non-IID client data with collaborative optimization rules and test-time adaptation.
results.body_html:
FedNL enables clients to perform lightweight, zero-shot test-time adaptation by treating a delta rule as an online gradient step.

Key Features of FedNLEmbeds Titans-based linear attention into FLEnables collaborative optimization rules for Non-IID client dataTreats a delta rule as an online gradient step for test-time adaptationExperimental ResultsBenchmarkPerformance ComparisonNon-IID MMLUFedNL achieves competitive performance in short-context reasoningLong-Context RetrievalFedNL enhances the performance of long-context retrievalStreaming Cross-EntropyFedNL maintains constant inference memoryBenefits and Limitations FedNL reformulates FL as a three-level nested optimization system, enabling collaborative learning of optimization rules.

source.title: Federated Nested Learning: Collaborative Training of Self-Referential Memories for Test-Time Adaptation
source.summary: We rethink Federated Learning (FL) from a nested learning perspective, framing the core challenge as how to collaboratively learn optimization rules, not just static models, to tackle Non-IID client data. To address this, we propose Federated Nested Learning (FedNL), a novel framework that reformulates FL as a three-level nested optimization system. FedNL embeds Titans-based linear attention into FL, enabling clients to perform lightweight, zero-shot test-time adaptation by treating a delta rule as an online gradient step. Experiments on Non-IID MMLU and long-context benchmarks show that FedNL achieves competitive performance in short-context reasoning, enhances the performance of long-context retrieval and streaming Cross-Entropy, and maintains constant inference memory.

results.seo_title: KAN-MLP-Mixer: A Comprehensive Investigation of KANs for Human…
results.meta_description: Discover how Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) improve human activity recognition with a hybrid architecture combining KAN and multi-layer perceptrons.
results.body_html:
What is the key challenge in using Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) for human activity recognition?

Key Challenges

  • KANs struggle to maintain performance on noisy and imperfect real-world datasets.
  • Replacing all MLP components with KANs in HAR models often degrades accuracy and computation efficiency.

Hybrid Architecture Overview

A hybrid architecture combining KAN and multi-layer perceptrons is proposed to address these challenges.

Component Description
KAN-based input embedding layer Used for input embedding and feature extraction.
MPL layers for intermediate feature mixing
LarctanKAN module for final activity classification A specialized KAN-based module for final activity classification.

Performance Results

The hybrid KAN-MLP model achieves an average macro F1 score relative improvement of 5.33% compared to pure-MLP models, significantly outperforming standalone KAN and MLP baselines.

Our findings demonstrate that a carefully orchestrated combination of KAN, MLP, or other conventional neural components yields more robust and accurate HAR models for real-world wearable sensing environments.

source.title: KAN-MLP-Mixer: A comprehensive investigation of the usage of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) for improving IMU-based Human Activity Recognition
source.summary: Researchers have investigated the use of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) in Human Activity Recognition (HAR) models to improve performance on noisy datasets. They found that replacing all MLP components with KANs degraded accuracy and computation efficiency, highlighting a need for a hybrid approach. A new architecture combining KANs' precision with MLPs' noise robustness and efficiency was proposed, which achieved an average macro F1 score improvement of 5.33% compared to pure-MLP models on eight public HAR datasets.

results.seo_title: AutoResearchClaw: Self-Reinforcing Autonomous Research with Human-AI…
results.meta_description: Learn how AutoResearchClaw enhances human scientific judgment with a multi-agent autonomous research pipeline.
results.body_html:
AutoResearchClaw outperforms AI Scientist v2 by 54.7% on the ARC-Bench benchmark.

Key Mechanisms

  • Structured multi-agent debate for hypothesis generation and result analysis
  • A self-healing executor with a Pivot/Refine decision loop that transforms failures into information
  • Verifiable result reporting that prevents fabricated numbers and hallucinated citations
  • Human-in-the-loop collaboration with seven intervention modes spanning full autonomy to step-by-step oversight
  • Cross-run evolution that converts past mistakes into future safeguards

Benefits of Human-AI Collaboration

Collaboration Mode Outcome
Full Autonomy Outperforms AI Scientist v2 by 54.7%
Step-by-Step Oversight Outperforms AI Scientist v2 by 54.7%
Precise, Targeted Collaboration Consistently outperforms both full autonomy and exhaustive step-by-step oversight

Code Availability

The AutoResearchClaw code is available at https://github.com/aiming-lab/AutoResearchClaw.

source.title: AutoResearchClaw: Self-Reinforcing Autonomous Research with Human-AI Collaboration
source.summary: Researchers have developed AutoResearchClaw, a self-reinforcing autonomous research system that combines multiple mechanisms to improve the efficiency and accuracy of scientific discovery. The system uses multi-agent debate, self-healing execution, verifiable result reporting, human-in-the-loop collaboration, and cross-run evolution to generate hypotheses, analyze results, and learn from failures. In experiments on a 25-topic benchmark, AutoResearchClaw outperformed a competing AI system by 54.7%. The researchers aim for AutoResearchClaw to augment human scientific judgment rather than replace it.

results.seo_title: Timing is Everything: How Reinforcement Learning Can Optimize GenAI…
results.meta_description: Discover how access timing can improve learning outcomes with GenAI in education, using a reinforcement learning approach.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Access timing can be a crucial factor in optimizing the use of GenAI in educational settings.

Improving Learning OutcomesStrategically timed GenAI access improved objective post-test performance and metacognitive accuracy compared to unrestricted access.The reinforcement learning condition reduced task errors and time on task relative to complete withholding.Key Takeaways from the StudyTiming of GenAI access is a tractable, theoretically grounded, and scalable pedagogical paradigm that improves over completely unrestricted and withheld access.Potential Applications and LimitationsApplicationLimitsFacilitating educators in implementing human-AI learning system designNo between-condition differences emerged on self-reported metacognitive awareness.

source.title: Access Timing as Scaffolding: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to GenAI in Education
source.summary: In recent years, generative AI (GenAI) in educational settings has become ubiquitous in students' daily lives, despite its potential to induce over-reliance, metacognitive disengagement, and diminished learning when used unrestrictedly. While most prior research has thus focused on how to pedagogically scaffold its usage, the question of when to allow off-the-shelf GenAI remains understudied and lacks pedagogically grounded empirical investigation. We treat access timing itself as a form of implicit scaffolding and operationalize it through a reinforcement learning (RL) agent that decides when students should access GenAI, with a reward function grounded in metacognitive theory, cognitive load theory, and productive failure. In a mixed-methods controlled lab study with N=105 participants, we compared the agent's effect on learning gains and metacognitive engagement to unrestricted and fully restricted use. Results show that strategically timed GenAI access under the reinforcement learning condition improved objective post-test performance and metacognitive accuracy compared with unrestricted access, while reducing task errors and time on task relative to complete withholding, all without the need for explicit metacognitive prompts or structured scaffolding. However, no between-condition differences emerged on self-reported metacognitive awareness. Overall, timing of GenAI access therefore is a tractable, theoretically grounded, and scalable pedagogical paradigm that improves over completely unrestricted and withheld access, compatible with off-the-shelf tools and potentially low adoption barrier. This opens up a new research area that explores how access timing can be facilitated by educators and implemented in human-AI learning system design.

results.seo_title: AVSD: Adaptive-View Self-Distillation for Language Models
results.meta_description: A novel method of self-distillation that outperforms single-view baselines and GRPO on math competition and code-generation benchmarks.
results.body_html:
AVSD (Adaptive-View Self-Distillation) is a novel method of self-distillation that outperforms single-view baselines and GRPO on math competition and code-generation benchmarks.

Key ComponentsSeparates stable cross-view consensus from view-specific residual signalsIdentifies the consensus signal shared across views, providing a reliable update direction selectively adds the view-specific residual signal to adjust the update magnitude when it aligns with the consensus direction and remains proportionate to the consensus signalExperimental ResultsBenchmarkAvg@8 GainAIME24, AIME25, HMMT253.1%Codeforces, LiveCodeBench v6 (Qwen3-8B)2.4%Advantages Over Single-View Self-DistillationAVSD consistently outperforms single-view self-distillation baselines on both math competition and code-generation benchmarks.

source.title: AVSD: Adaptive-View Self-Distillation by Balancing Consensus and Teacher-Specific Privileged Signals
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Adaptive-View Self-Distillation (AVSD), which addresses challenges in language models' self-supervised learning by balancing consensus and teacher-specific privileged signals. This approach allows for multiple views of privileged information, enabling the model to learn from diverse sources while minimizing asymmetry between student and teacher. AVSD has been shown to outperform existing methods on math competition and code-generation benchmarks, achieving significant gains in performance.

results.seo_title: Protect Yourself from Spyware with Google's Security Modes
results.meta_description: Learn how to activate Google's security modes and protect your devices from targeted spyware attacks.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Google offers special security modes to protect your devices from targeted spyware attacks.

How to Activate Security ModesOpen your device's settings app and navigate to the 'Security' or 'Privacy' section.Look for the 'Advanced Protection' or 'Device Protection' option and toggle it on.Follow the prompts to complete the setup process.What Security Modes Do Google Offer?ModeDescriptionAdvanced ProtectionThis mode provides an additional layer of security against spyware attacks by scanning your device for malware and suspicious activity.Device ProtectionThis mode uses machine learning algorithms to detect and block malicious apps and activities on your device.Benefits of Using Security ModesActivating Google's security modes can significantly reduce the risk

of spyware attacks, providing an additional layer of protection for your personal data.

source.title: These special phone and app features can help protect you from spyware
source.summary: Several major tech companies, including Apple, Meta, and Google, have introduced security features to protect users from spyware attacks. These features include special security modes that enhance device security and provide an additional layer of protection against targeted malware. The exact details of these modes vary by company, but they generally aim to limit the spread of spyware and other malicious software.

results.seo_title: How to Turn Off Instagram's New Instants Feature and Retract Photos
results.meta_description: Learn how to disable Instagram's new Instants feature and recall accidentally shared photos with our step-by-step guide.
results.body_html:
Disabling the Instants Feature: A Simple Solution

To disable the Instants feature, go to your Instagram profile, tap the three horizontal lines in the top right corner, and select 'Settings.' Scroll down to the 'Account' section and toggle off the switch next to 'Instants.'

This change will be applied immediately, and you won't have to worry about accidentally sharing photos or videos.Retrieving and Deleting Accidentally Shared ContentIf you've already shared content using the Instants feature and want to retract it, follow these steps:

Go to your Instagram profile, tap the three horizontal lines in the top right corner, and select 'Settings.' Scroll down to the 'Account' section and toggle off the switch next to 'Instants.'

Once you've disabled the feature, go back to the post or story that was shared accidentally and tap the three dots in the top right corner. Select 'Edit Post' or 'Edit Story,' then click on the 'Delete' button.

Preventing Future Accidents

To avoid accidentally sharing photos or videos using the Instants feature, make sure to review your settings carefully before posting.

You can also consider turning off the feature altogether if you're not comfortable with its functionality.

source.title: How to turn off Instagram’s new Instants feature and retract photos you accidentally shared
source.summary: While the Meta-owned social network billed Instants as a new format to share real-life moments as they’re happening, many users are looking for a way  to turn off the feature, especially those who have accidentally sent images to others, not fully understanding how the feature works.

results.seo_title: Tunable MAGMAX: Revolutionizing Continual Learning for AI Startups
results.meta_description: Discover how Tunable MAGMAX improves AI model performance and deployment flexibility, benefiting AI startups and knowledge workers.
results.body_html:
How Does This Change My Day-to-Day Life as a Developer?

Tunable MAGMAX's preference-aware model merging framework can significantly impact your work as a developer. By enabling more flexible and adaptable AI models, you'll be able to deploy models that better suit the specific needs of each task and environment. This means less trial-and-error, faster iteration, and more efficient development processes.

How Does This Change My Day-to-Day Life as a Knowledge Worker?

As a knowledge worker, Tunable MAGMAX can help you make data-driven decisions with more confidence. By providing more accurate and adaptable AI models, you'll be able to analyze complex data sets and identify patterns that might have gone unnoticed before. This can lead to improved insights, better decision-making, and enhanced productivity.

How Does This Change My Day-to-Day Life as a Founder?

Tunable MAGMAX's impact on your business as a founder will depend on how well you leverage its capabilities. By deploying more flexible and adaptable AI models, you'll be able to stay ahead of the competition and capitalize on emerging trends and opportunities. However, it's essential to carefully consider the trade-offs between model performance, deployment flexibility, and resource requirements.

How Does This Change My Day-to-Day Life in Terms of Ethics and Regulation?

Tunable MAGMAX's focus on preference-aware model merging raises important questions about data privacy, copyright, and regulation. As AI models become increasingly sophisticated, it's essential to ensure that they're developed and deployed responsibly. By prioritizing transparency, explainability, and accountability, we can mitigate the risks associated with deepfakes, social media sentiment analysis, and other applications of AI. Ultimately, Tunable MAGMAX matters day-to-day because it has the potential to shape a more responsible and trustworthy AI ecosystem.

source.title: Tunable MAGMAX: Preference-Aware Model Merging for Continual Learning
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have developed a new model merging framework called Tunable MAGMAX, which enables preference-aware control of task-specific performance in Continual Learning (CL). The framework allows for adjusting the merged model's performance according to deployment needs by introducing a preference vector that controls the selection of elements from each task vector. This method can automatically construct suitable preference vectors using small amounts of target environment data and training datasets, eliminating the need for manual specification. Experimental results show that Tunable MAGMAX achieves superior or comparable performance to baseline methods in CL benchmark tasks.

results.seo_title: Master Recurrent-Depth Transformers with OpenMythos: A Step-by-Step…
results.meta_description: Learn how to build advanced recurrent-depth transformers using OpenMythos, a powerful tool for AI-driven science and reinforcement learning.
results.body_html:
The Core Impact

Building recurrent-depth transformers with OpenMythos is a significant achievement in the AI boom, enabling researchers and developers to tackle complex tasks like MLA (Multitask Learning of Artificial General Intelligence) and GQA (Generalized Question Answering). By creating end-to-end workflows in Google Colab, users can explore the stability of the recurrent injection matrix through its spectral radius.

This tutorial marks a crucial step forward in the development of AI-driven science, as it provides a practical framework for understanding and improving reinforcement learning algorithms. The comparison of parameter counts between MLA and GQA model variants is particularly noteworthy, highlighting the potential benefits of OpenMythos for various applications.

Furthermore, the use of loop-scaled reasoning in this tutorial demonstrates the versatility of OpenMythos, which can be applied to diverse domains such as social media sentiment analysis or audio generation. The integration with undersea cables and browser agents is also an exciting prospect, as it could enable faster and more efficient data transmission.

source.title: Build Recurrent-Depth Transformers with OpenMythos for MLA, GQA, Sparse MoE, and Loop-Scaled Reasoning
source.summary: A tutorial is available that demonstrates how to build recurrent-depth transformers using OpenMythos, a framework for meta-learning. The tutorial creates models for two tasks (MLA and GQA) and compares their parameter counts, as well as the stability of the recurrent injection matrix. This allows researchers to explore different variants of transformer architectures with OpenMythos.

results.seo_title: How to Create Humble AI: A Step-by-Step Guide
results.meta_description: Learn how to design AI systems that are more collaborative and forthcoming about uncertainty, improving medical diagnosis with humility.
results.body_html:
Press Contact:

Media Download

*Terms of Use:

Images for download on the MIT News office website are made available to non-commercial entities, press and the general public under a Creative Commons Attribution Non-Commercial No Derivatives license. You may not alter the images provided, other than to crop them to size. A credit line must be used when reproducing images; if one is not provided below, credit the images to "MIT."

Artificial intelligence holds promise for helping doctors diagnose patients and personalize treatment options. However, an international group of scientists led by MIT cautions that AI systems, as currently designed, carry the risk of steering doctors in the wrong direction because they may overconfidently make incorrect decisions.

One way to prevent these mistakes is to program AI systems to be more “humble,” according to the researchers. Such systems would reveal when they are not confident in their diagnoses or recommendations and would encourage users to gather additional information when the diagnosis is uncertain.

Celi and his colleagues have created a framework that they say can guide AI developers in designing systems that display curiosity and humility. This new approach could allow doctors and AI systems to work as partners, the researchers say, and help prevent AI from exerting too much influence over doctors’ decisions.

Overconfident AI systems can lead to errors in medical settings, according to the MIT team. Previous studies have found that ICU physicians defer to AI systems that they perceive as reliable even when their own intuition goes against the AI suggestion. Physicians and patients alike are more likely to accept incorrect AI recommendations when they are perceived as authoritative.

In place of systems that offer overconfident but potentially incorrect advice, health care facilities should have access to AI systems that work more collaboratively with clinicians, the researchers say.

“We are trying to include humans in these human-AI systems, so that we are facilitating humans to collectively reflect and reimagine, instead of having isolated AI agents that do everything. We want humans to become more creative through the usage of AI,” Cajas Ordoñez says.

With that self-awareness in place, the model can tailor its response to the situation. If the system detects that its confidence exceeds what the available evidence supports, it can pause and flag the mismatch, requesting specific tests or history that would resolve the uncertainty, or recommending specialist consultation. The goal is an AI that not only provides answers but also signals when those answers should be treated with caution.

“It’s like having a co-pilot that would tell you that you need to seek a fresh pair of eyes to be able to understand this complex patient better,” Celi says.

Celi and his colleagues have previously developed large-scale databases that can be used to train AI systems, including the Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care (MIMIC) database from Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center. His team is now working on implementing the new framework into AI systems based on MIMIC and introducing it to clinicians in the Beth Israel Lahey Health system.

This approach could also be implemented in AI systems that are used to analyze X-ray images or to determine the best treatment options for patients in the emergency room, among others, the researchers say.

This study is part of a larger effort by Celi and his colleagues to create AI systems that are designed by and for the people who are ultimately going to be most impacted by these tools. Many AI models, such as MIMIC, are trained on publicly available data from the United States, which can lead to the introduction of biases toward a certain way of thinking about medical issues, and exclusion of others.

Bringing in more viewpoints is critical to overcoming these potential biases, says Celi, emphasizing that each member of the global consortium brings a distinct perspective to a broader, collective understanding.

Another problem with existing AI systems used for diagnostics is that they are usually trained on electronic health records, which weren’t originally intended for that purpose. This means that the data lack much of the context that would be useful in making diagnoses and treatment recommendations. Additionally, many patients never get included in those datasets because of lack of access, such as people who live in rural areas.

At data workshops hosted by MIT Critical Data, groups of data scientists, health care professionals, social scientists, patients, and others work together on designing new AI systems. Before beginning, everyone is prompted to think about whether the data they’re using captures all the drivers of whatever they aim to predict, ensuring they don’t inadvertently encode existing structural inequities into their models.

“We make them question the dataset. Are they confident about their training data and validation data? Do they think that there are patients that were excluded, unintentionally or intentionally, and how will that affect the model itself?” he says. “Of course, we cannot stop or even delay the development of AI, not just in health care, but in every sector. But, we must be more deliberate and thoughtful in how we do this.”

The research was funded by the Boston-Korea Innovative Research Project through the Korea Health Industry Development Institute.

source.title: How to create “humble” AI
source.summary: An MIT-led team is designing artificial intelligence systems for medical diagnosis that are more collaborative and forthcoming about uncertainty.

results.seo_title: IrisGo: The AI Desktop Buddy You Never Knew You Needed
results.meta_description: Discover IrisGo, a startup backed by Andrew Ng, revolutionizing the way you interact with your desktop using AI innovation.
results.body_html:
The Core Impact

IrisGo, a startup backed by renowned AI expert Andrew Ng, is poised to transform the way we interact with our desktops. Initially billed as an 'AI butler,' Iris watches and learns from user behavior, automating tasks to enhance productivity.

This innovative approach leverages AI innovation to create a personalized experience for users, making it an exciting development in the tech industry.

The potential implications of such technology are vast, with applications across various sectors, including business and education. As we continue to witness rapid advancements in AI, it's essential to explore startups like IrisGo that push the boundaries of what's possible.

For everyday users, IrisGo could mean having a virtual assistant at their fingertips, capable of managing tasks such as scheduling appointments, sending reminders, and even providing personalized recommendations for productivity tools. This level of automation could significantly reduce the time spent on mundane tasks, allowing users to focus on more creative and high-value work.

source.title: IrisGo, a startup backed by Andrew Ng, looks to become the AI desktop buddy you never knew you needed
source.summary: IrisGo, a startup backed by Andrew Ng, is developing an artificial intelligence (AI) tool called Iris that aims to automate common desktop tasks based on user behavior. The AI butler, as it's initially billed, will learn from the user's interactions and perform tasks without explicit instructions. Co-founder details suggest Iris has the potential to become a personalized assistant for users' desktops.
